title: Calappa martiana
intro: Calappa martiana, also known as Sea Cucumber or Calappa, is a type of marine plant that belongs to the family Calappidae. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Calappa martiana include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Calappa martiana include teas, infusions, and capsules, which are often used to support joint health, improve skin conditions, and enhance overall well-being.
taxonomy: Calappa martiana is classified in the kingdom Animalia, due to it being a marine animal, and more specifically in the phylum Arthropoda. uses: Calappa martiana, a species of turtle shell crab, is not typically known for its medicinal uses.
